name: test-framework-scaffold
description: Test directory structure (helpers/, integration/, e2e/, load/) with TestClient HTTP wrapper, MockModel LLM simulator, and budget-capped test suites. Jest with ts-jest, workspace integration via root package.json workspaces array.

name: in-memory-dynamodb-test-client
description: In-memory DynamoDB test client pattern: Implements full DynamoDB interface (query, get, put, update, delete, scan) using Map-based storage. Handles: (1) PK/SK composite keys via string concatenation, (2) query() with KeyConditionExpression and FilterExpression, (3) scan() with FilterExpression parsing, (4) update() with UpdateExpression parsing (use_count increment, timestamp updates). Allows testing multi-tenant isolation, GSI queries, and security filters without AWS SDK dependency or LocalStack.

name: mcp-integration-test-pattern
description: Comprehensive MCP Gateway testing requires mock HTTP client for API calls, mock S3 for bundle storage, and mock registry for skill metadata. Tests cover 5 trust levels (platform, verified, community, experimental, private) with distinct sandbox configs and Cedar policies. Integration tests validate end-to-end flows: skill installation → MCP registration → agent tool loading → invocation.

content: MCP test file structure: mcp-gateway-client.test.ts (18 tests - registration, sandbox, Cedar), installer.test.ts (13 tests - install/uninstall/update workflows), agent-skills.test.ts (5 integration tests - end-to-end flows). Mock classes follow interface pattern: MockHttpClient implements HttpClient, MockS3 implements S3Client, MockRegistry provides getSkill/getSkillInstall/recordInstall methods.

Multi-tenant test pattern: (1) Mock DynamoDB client with interface matching real client, (2) createMockTenantConfig() helper generates test tenant data, (3) createMockJWT() builds Base64url-encoded JWT tokens, (4) Cross-tenant isolation tests verify user A cannot access tenant B resources, (5) Test helpers in test-helpers.ts shared across test files.

name: test-isolation-from-peerdependencies
description: When testing modules with peerDependencies (AWS SDK, Strands SDK), focus tests on pure logic functions (retry, error formatting, query builders) that don't require runtime imports. Use inline implementations in tests where necessary to avoid import failures. Structure: (1) Test utility functions separately from client classes, (2) Mock external dependencies at function boundaries, (3) Verify constants and type definitions inline. This allows meaningful test coverage without installing all peerDependencies in worktree environments.

name: test-type-isolation-pattern
description: Tests for AWS SDK-dependent modules should import only types (not implementations) to avoid loading SDK dependencies. Use 'import type' for all exports from index files to prevent SDK module resolution errors. This enables testing type structures, data validation, and tenant isolation without requiring AWS SDK installation.

name: cdk-assertion-testing-pattern
description: CDK tests use Template.fromStack() for synthesis, resourceCountIs() for counts, hasResourceProperties() for property validation. CloudFormation intrinsic functions (Fn::Join) require Match.objectLike() or omitting property checks.

name: cdk-test-environment-differences
description: Test both dev and prod configurations in separate describe blocks. Key differences: NAT gateway count (1 vs 2), log retention (30d vs 365d), removal policies (DESTROY vs RETAIN).

name: weak-assertion-strengthening
description: Replace .toBeDefined() with type checks (typeof x === 'string'), length checks (.length > 0), and specific property validations. Example: expect(sessionId).toBeDefined() → expect(typeof sessionId).toBe('string') + expect(sessionId.length).toBeGreaterThan(0).

content: test-file-per-module-convention: Each core module under packages/core/src/ should have a __tests__/ directory containing at minimum one test file. Test files use bun:test (import { describe, it, expect } from 'bun:test'). Mock AWS SDK v3 clients rather than making real API calls. Use meaningful assertions (not standalone .toBeDefined() or expect(true).toBe(true)). CDK stack tests go in infra/test/ using Template.fromStack with hasResourceProperties/resourceCountIs.

name: test-coverage-parallel-builder-decomposition
description: 14 untested modules covered in parallel via 4 builders with non-overlapping file scope: (1) core-small: 6 small modules, (2) aws-discovery: 2 large modules, (3) orch-swarm: 4 orchestration-adjacent modules, (4) cdk-fixes: CDK assertion tests + weak assertion hardening. Group by complexity similarity, not just module count.

name: bun-test-bedrock-env
description: Bun test ignores jest.config.ts. Use BEDROCK_ENABLED=false prefix to disable external service clients in tests.

name: slack-url-verification-order
description: Slack URL verification must bypass tenant middleware. Add route-specific handler checking req.body.type === 'url_verification' BEFORE extractTenantContext.

content: quality-gate-test-baseline: After full dep install, test suite runs 979/990 (98.9%). 7 failures in unimplemented features (admin API CRUD, streaming endpoints). Chat-gateway tests need BEDROCK_ENABLED=false env var and NODE_ENV=test set in jest.setup.ts. Rate limiter tests need proper DynamoDB mock setup.

cdk-test-pipeline-stack-beforeall: PipelineStack CDK tests must use beforeAll (not beforeEach) because the stack contains 4 inline Python Lambda functions + Step Functions + CodePipeline — synthesis takes 15-30s. Using beforeEach causes bun test timeout failures. Pattern: declare 'let template: Template' at describe scope, initialize in beforeAll with self-contained app/stack creation.

cdk-test-beforeall-pattern: CDK test files using expensive stack synthesis (many Lambdas with logRetention, custom resources) must use beforeAll not beforeEach. Bun test has a 5s per-test timeout including hooks. Stacks with logRetention triggers CDK LogRetentionFunction singleton creation which adds ~3-5s to synthesis. Using beforeAll synthesizes the stack once per describe block.

description: cdk-secretsmanager-grantreadwrite-missing: aws-cdk-lib/aws-secretsmanager.Secret in v2.244.0 does NOT have grantReadWrite() method. Only grantRead() and grantWrite() are available. Code calling secret.grantReadWrite() throws TypeError.
resolution: Use secret.grantRead(fn) and secret.grantWrite(fn) separately. Fix infra/lib/skill-pipeline-stack.ts line 174: replace signingKeySecret.grantReadWrite(fn) with signingKeySecret.grantRead(fn) and signingKeySecret.grantWrite(fn).

content: hono-test-createAdaptorServer-pattern: Route tests for Hono routers must use createAdaptorServer({fetch:app.fetch}) from @hono/node-server for supertest compatibility. Context injection via app.use('path/*', async (c,next) => { (c as any).set(key,val); await next(); }). Router mounting via app.route('/prefix', router). For auth context use c.set('auth',...) not c.set('tenantContext',...) when route reads c.get('auth'). Middleware mocks need Hono signature: async (c,next) => await next(), not (req,res,next) => next().

cdk-test-lambda-count-drift: TenantOnboardingStack test assertions for Lambda count (9) and StateMachine count (1) became stale when offboarding workflow was added. Stack now has 15 app Lambdas + 1 CDK LogRetention = 16, and 2 state machines (onboarding + offboarding). Pattern: CDK test resourceCountIs assertions must be updated whenever the stack adds new resources.

evolution-module-test-strategy: When testing ModelRouter/AutoSkillGenerator, static-mode selectModel and generateSkillFromPattern don't call DynamoDB so test directly. For DynamoDB paths use mock.module('@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb'). All mock QueryCommandOutput/GetCommandOutput must include $metadata field or TypeScript errors occur.

skill-registry-mock-fixture: SkillRegistry DynamoDBClient interface (registry.ts) requires methods returning QueryCommandOutput/GetCommandOutput etc. with $metadata. Skill type uses snake_case fields (trust_level, created_at, updated_at) and requires PK/SK keys. InMemoryDynamoDBClient mock must implement all 6 methods: get/put/update/delete/query/scan with $metadata in every return.

jest-to-bun-migration-pattern: Remove jest/ts-jest/@types/jest from devDeps, change test script to 'bun test', delete jest.config.ts files. Replace jest.fn() with mock(), jest.mock(path,factory) with mock.module(path,factory), jest.spyOn() with spyOn(). Store mock functions as named variables before mock.module() for per-test overrides. ESM hoisting prevents jest.mock()-style hoisting for relative imports in bun -- use mock.module() before the module loads. CDK tests: create bunfig.toml with [test] timeout=30000 instead of jest.setTimeout(). Integration/e2e tests with @jest/globals: simple import swap to bun:test.

bun-mock-module-named-variables: In bun:test, store mock functions as named variables BEFORE calling mock.module() to enable per-test overrides. Pattern: const mockFn = mock(() => defaultVal); mock.module('path', () => ({ fn: mockFn })); then in tests: mockFn.mockReturnValueOnce(...). This replaces jest's (x as jest.Mock).mockReturnValueOnce() pattern and avoids type casting.

bun integration test skip pattern: when tests require external service (CHIMERA_TEST_API_URL), add guard at top of describe block: if (\!process.env.VAR) { test.skip('skipped: VAR not set', () => {}); return; }

macos-tmpdir-symlink-realpath: On macOS, os.tmpdir() returns /var/... but process.cwd() after chdir resolves /private/var/... — use fs.realpathSync() when creating tmp dirs for tests that compare against cwd-based paths.

name: strands-tool-decorator-noop-for-testing
description: In pytest tests for Strands proxy tools, patch @tool decorator with mocker.patch('gateway_proxy.tool', side_effect=lambda f: f) to make it a no-op. This returns the raw closure from create_gateway_proxy_tool(), making it directly callable with **kwargs for Lambda payload assertions without needing the full Strands FunctionTool wrapper.

content: worktree-test-failures-are-environment: Test failures in fresh worktrees are almost always from missing node_modules. Verify with Cannot find package errors before attributing to code bugs.
